mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth, compressing corporate profit margins and depressing equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are not significantly negative when UNRATE drops below 5.0 percent, or positive returns dominate in more than 60 percent of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous surges in industrial production and housing starts indicate broad-based economic acceleration that outpaces capacity constraints, driving earnings revisions higher and lifting equity prices over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50 percent of observations over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising rate environment, real GDP growth exceeding 3.0 percent annualized signals resilient corporate demand that offsets higher financing costs, producing positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative or flat when GDPC1 growth exceeds 3.0 percent during FEDFUNDS hikes, contradicting the positive direction prediction.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly payroll gains exceeding 150,000 jobs reflect robust aggregate demand expansion that supports corporate revenue trajectories and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: Weekly forward returns do not exhibit a positive skew when monthly payroll gains exceed 150,000 jobs, or the signal produces negative returns in more than 55 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer credit outstanding expands alongside stable inflation, household balance sheet strength fuels discretionary spending, driving earnings upgrades and positive equity returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising RSAFS and stable CPIAUCSL fails to produce positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of constrained industrial output growth, any acceleration in manufacturing activity triggers short-covering rallies and upward earnings revisions, lifting equity prices over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth rate crosses from negative to positive
failure_condition: Equity returns are not significantly positive following INDPRO acceleration from sub-zero growth, or negative returns dominate in more than 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising residential housing starts reflect improving consumer confidence and lower mortgage rates, which historically precede broad-based equity outperformance over a weekly trading window.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no positive relationship with HOUST increases, or the Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all rolling windows.
